{{Infobox concept
| name = Aether
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Aether Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Aether is a fundamental, omnipresent force that exists throughout the universe, permeating physical reality and even extending into the vacuum of space. It functions as a cosmic current that reflects the shape of the galaxy and serves as the essential medium for all magic; spells do not require air, but rather rely on the Aether to manifest. It is theorized that this energy may leak from other dimensions into this one. The Aether can be manipulated to create various phenomena, including elemental constructs like [[dragons]] or serpents, energy bolts, and complex defensive structures. It is also used in advanced technology known as [[Aethertech]], which includes legendary [[Zenith Devices]] and specialized engines like the [[Aether Drive]]. Beyond its role in magic and technology, the Aether has profound biological and planetary effects. It can sustain habitable atmospheres by facilitating the development of [[World Spirits]], and it can influence planetary environments, such as driving volcanic activity. The Aether also interacts with living beings; it can reflect a person's vitality, respond to their intentions, and even seem to react to or prepare individuals for upcoming threats. Spirits themselves are considered manifestations of the Aether rather than biological entities. While some believe the Aether possesses a form of awareness or purpose, others view it as a natural force that can be read by those with specific magical affinities.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The Aether is a sentient force and magical medium that can grant a person two lives or bring possible realities into existence. This energy, which may consist of leaks from other worlds, can be manipulated through symbols, natural patterns, or by resonating the meaning of words to manifest spells. While it can be channeled through staves, devices, or even used to conjure water and dark water, the Aether is also affected by an individual's resolve and self-definition. During massive conflicts, the force churns like a stormy sea, yet it can also be used to create protective seals or move individuals through the [[void]]. Ultimately, mages become more powerful as they grow closer to this medium, which can answer requests for magic and control.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] can decide for a person to live two lives.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c1">''The Captain'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] is a force that twisted the entire sky during the ritual.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c2">''The Captain'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] is a source or medium related to magic.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] can be manipulated via symbols.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Spells require [[Aether]] to manifest.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3">''The Captain'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The protagonist can disrupt the [[Aether]] around weapons.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Original spells were derived from natural patterns in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c4">''The Captain'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator can nudge the [[Aether]] to push it into specific patterns.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Spells are cast by causing the meaning of words to resonate with the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c5">''The Captain'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] is a medium through which trigger words can echo.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c6">''The Captain'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] can be affected by resolve.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Horizon]]'s projected form is a reflection of her identity and emotions through the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] can pause or witness moments.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c9">''The Captain'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The protagonist can call upon the [[Aether]] to create sparks from their fingertips.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11">''The Captain'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] can be twisted into intricate designs.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] flowed between the ships to form a seal.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A word can shake the [[Aether]] and deconstruct a spell.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c13">''The Captain'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] has a will.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c14">''The Captain'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] churns like a sea in storm during massive conflicts.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Symbols can flicker in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Shepherd]] can draw up [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18">''The Captain'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Wizards swear on the [[Aether]], though it is not a [[binding]] vow.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21">''The Captain'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] is a force that defines magic users based on how they define themselves.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c22">''The Captain'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Water can be conjured from the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c23">''The Captain'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] is a sense used to detect the power of bullets.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c24">''The Captain'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator calls upon power from the [[Aether]] using an incantation.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c27">''The Captain'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] hovers on a flow of [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28">''The Captain'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Skimmer Drive]] produces a steady hum of [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30">''The Captain'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] is a form of energy that can flow through devices.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c31">''The Captain'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] can be poured as water and used to spin in rings around hands.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c31" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] is a medium through which sound can be carried.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c32">''The Captain'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Dark water can be drawn from the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c36">''The Captain'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Last Horizon]] is gathering [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c37">''The Captain'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] is a source of energy used for conjuring protective seals.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38">''The Captain'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] is a medium through which possible lives can be brought into reality.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39">''The Captain'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|There is a theory that the [[Aether]] consists of energy leaking from other worlds.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] is a concept or entity mentioned in conversation between the narrator and [[Omal]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40">''The Captain'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator can wrestle water gushing from the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42">''The Captain'', chapter 42</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri]] can command the [[Aether]] to move himself through the [[void]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c43">''The Captain'', chapter 43</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mages are more closely tied to the [[Aether]] the more powerful they are.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44">''The Captain'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Magic is tied to an individual's definition in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45">''The Captain'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Aether]] can answer requests for magic and control.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]] can be added to spells to introduce chaos.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46">''The Captain'', chapter 46</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator channeled [[Aether]] through their staff.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46" />}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Though often insulted by the Advocates, the Aether is a complex current reflecting the shape of the galaxy that can be manipulated to conjure [[dragons]] or red fire. This substance, which may be dispersed by proprietary alloys and can be thin or nonexistent in certain spaces, serves as the essential support for [[Raion]]'s [[Combat Art]] and is required to operate truth spells. The Aether responds to [[Starhammer]]'s desire for power by providing memories of alternate selves, yet it also acts as a force that can lead people and prevents things from being too easy. While the [[Zenith Device]] bends this function to break the laws of reality, the Aether remains a force that experiences warps and holds the sole knowledge of the outcome of [[Starhammer]]'s battle.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] is a state of being that the Advocates insult.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c1">''The Engineer'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether]] can be manipulated to conjure [[dragons]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c4">''The Engineer'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether]] is a substance that can be dispersed by proprietary alloys.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c5">''The Engineer'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion]]'s [[Combat Art]] requires the support of the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] can be commanded to sweep an area clean.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c12">''The Engineer'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Karoshan]]'s [[force-blade]] block invokes the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14">''The Engineer'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] responds to [[Starhammer]]'s desire for power.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16">''The Engineer'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] can provide memories of alternate selves.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] can tremble.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] is a force that can lead people somewhere.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c25">''The Engineer'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether]] is something one can swear upon.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c27">''The Engineer'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] prevents things from being too easy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] is a current that reflects the shape of the galaxy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Zenith Device]] bends the [[Aether]] to break the laws of reality.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Red]] fire can be conjured from the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c37">''The Engineer'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether]] is a function that supersedes natural logic.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39">''The Engineer'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether]] can experience warps.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40">''The Engineer'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Measuring behavior of the [[Aether]] is not exact.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Leilari]] experiences a twist of the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|An android's appearance may be determined by its resonance in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41">''The Engineer'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The heart of any [[Combat Art]] is a truth embedded in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c44">''The Engineer'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Only the [[Aether]] knows the outcome of [[Starhammer]]'s battle.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45">''The Engineer'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether]] can be thin or nonexistent in certain spaces.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Cutting off the [[Aether]] affects both [[Horizon]]'s systems and spells.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] can be naturally thinner in some places.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48">''The Engineer'', chapter 48</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] does not waste details.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50">''The Engineer'', chapter 50</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Aether]] is required to operate truth spells.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator uses the [[Aether]] for telekinetic leaps.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Greth]]'s species is blessed by the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55">''The Engineer'', chapter 55</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The Aether serves as a medium through which [[life]] energy is reflected as power and vitality, capable of churning like a stormy sea when magical orders conflict. This force can be heard through sounds like shattering porcelain, can respond to the wishes of [[Raion]], and possesses the power to awaken [[Zenith Devices]] independently. While wizards work with it directly and Archmages explore its depths, Elementalists use the Aether to conjure and control various elements. Although it can bend [[fate]] and resonates with specific objects, the Aether grows weak within the [[Shadow Ark]]. Ultimately, this resource can be read by the narrator and has been used to teach Kyri lessons and prepare the [[Captain]] for upcoming threats.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Visiri]] third eye sees [[life]] energy as a reflection of power and vitality through the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c1">''The Knight'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] can be churned and frothing like a sea in storm during conflicting magical orders.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3">''The Knight'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aether]] is a medium through which sounds like shattering porcelain can be heard.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] is a concept or entity that can hear [[Raion]]'s wishes.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c6">''The Knight'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] can cause [[Zenith Devices]] to awaken on their own.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c8">''The Knight'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] has been teaching Kyri hard lessons.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c12">''The Knight'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] is a force that can be worked with directly by wizards.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16">''The Knight'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] has things in store for the crew.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c19">''The Knight'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aether]] is a resource found on certain planets.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23">''The Knight'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Elementalists conjure and control elements from the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Archmages explore the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Wizards can see the evidence of [[Aether]] moving.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28">''The Knight'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] answers when the [[Captain]] asks about worst-case scenarios.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c33">''The Knight'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Blue Divine Titan]] amplifies acts of [[Aether manipulation]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c34">''The Knight'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator can read the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35">''The Knight'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aether]] can bend [[fate]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36">''The Knight'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] is a medium that resonates with certain objects.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] is weak inside [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c41">''The Knight'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] was bending against the narrator.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c43">''The Knight'', chapter 43</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Aether]] prepared the [[Captain]] for future threats.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c45">''The Knight'', chapter 45</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Aether is a powerful force invoked during the signing of contracts and can be read by specific individuals. It serves as a source of telekinetic force, responds to the anger of figures like the [[Captain]] and Shyrax through magical symbols or golden flames, and can even be used to freeze air or lock ritual items. This entity views individuals through the lens of their stories and actions, which has rendered the daughter of [[Omega]] the only identifiable member of [[Solstice]] to it. Beyond its role in combat arts and spells that require resonance, the Aether has awakened the [[Zenith Devices]] for a potential unified purpose and has been disturbed by an approaching apocalypse, causing it to shift as the narrator was redefined.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Signing the contract involved invoking the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c2">''The Pilot'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Melerius]] claims his prompting comes from the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c6">''The Pilot'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] can be read by certain individuals.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c7">''The Pilot'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] can be used to exert telekinetic force.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] in the room ignited with magical symbols when the [[Captain]] became angry.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16">''The Pilot'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omal]] swears on the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c17">''The Pilot'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] is mentioned in relation to tearing apart plans.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] responded to Shyrax's anger with golden yellow flames.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c21">''The Pilot'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] called for the [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c23">''The Pilot'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] may intend for the [[Zenith Devices]] to take up a unified purpose.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] awakened the [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Captain]] used [[Aether]] to freeze the air around her father's computer.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24">''The Pilot'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] sees individuals based on their story and actions.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] is making the daughter of [[Omega]] the only identifiable member of [[Solstice]] to him.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] is responsible for the inability to kill certain people.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aether]] was used to lock ritual items in place.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c34">''The Pilot'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] wishes to be baptized in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40">''The Pilot'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aethril eyes]] allow the user to see the flow of the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Combat Arts]] and spells both require actions that resonate with the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c45">''The Pilot'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|An approaching apocalyptic event has disturbed the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c48">''The Pilot'', chapter 48</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Aether]] shifted as the narrator was redefined.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c48" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“It’s the will of the Aether!” he exclaimed.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c14" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“It’s the genuine Zenith Starship, I swear on the Aether,” I said. That’s not a binding vow, but wizards were still wary of breaking it.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The “life energy” they see is a reflection of a person’s power and vitality through the Aether.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c1" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“Listen, Mister Escalon, I swear on the Aether and all the worlds that this doesn’t mean what you think!”<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“Perhaps the Aether means for us to take up that cause once again.”<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c23" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon concepts]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
